lesson.menu_desc: Ordering food with countable & uncountable nouns -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- Eating Out: Ordering at a Restaurant with some, any, much & many
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Pires | Aula 13 -- Eating Out at a Restaurant

listenings.text: Good evening, and welcome to our restaurant. Let me tell you about tonight's specials. As a starter, we have a fresh tomato soup. There are not many tables left, so I am glad you have a reservation. For the main course, the chef recommends grilled fish with a side dish of rice and vegetables. We do not have much beef tonight, but we have some lovely chicken. For dessert, there is a little chocolate cake left, and it is wonderful. If you need anything, just call the waiter. Enjoy your meal.

I love trying restaurants when I travel. The first time I ate out in a new country, I did not know how to read the menu, so I just pointed at the pictures. Now I always ask the waiter a few questions before I order. I ask if there is any fish, how much the main course costs, and what side dish comes with it. A good tip is to order a starter to share. And at the end, I always leave a tip for good service. Eating out is one of my favorite parts of any trip.

extra_audio.text: First, Roberto reads the menu and asks the waiter a few questions. Then he orders a starter and a main course with a side dish. Next, the waiter brings the food and asks if he needs anything else. After that, Roberto enjoys his dessert and a cup of coffee. Finally, he asks for the bill and leaves a tip for the good service.
